title: Betraying Dreams
content:
I used to have dreams about my life at the university
but those dreams lied to me.
They never showed me that the university is full of adversity,
Better days are what they showed me.
My dreams said the university is simple and amazing
But, actually, it is a maze.
I believed that the studies were going to be amusing
but what they actually do is to confuse.
I planed to be cooking delicious healthy meals
but cooking turned out to be illegal.
I feel betrayed by my own dreams
but I will keep on working hard to achieve my final goal.

title: SOCIETY
content:
My society my pride,
The source of all forces of life
My society the trajectory for everyone
Am in a society that people aren't always bad
The society that people aren't always good
The society that keeps me speechless
At its does, that can contradict young minds.
But, here's my society,
That has time for itself
That never care for others option
That wants everyone everyone to do one thing,
but never cares for unity,
That is full of envy, jealous… rather protesting love and self-justice,
The society rich of poverty
Where poverty is a law material for the rich
To have more for themselves a little for no one,
But we, exchange our energy for that little pay anyway
That's My society where justice is for money
The society that claims true for itself
Where it demands work after school, and marriage for a kid
But meet my society needs… are bygones now,
Brings no room to exercise its demands
But… if I deviate, you call me a robber, you call me a prostitute
Where are the marriages you demand?
Where is the job, why calling me a robber, for my living?
That's my society I enjoy…,
I live in a society…, the society I don't know.

title: Deception
content:
In shadows cast blissful smile
My heart aches from within itches
Deception flooded through my blinded eyes
In my mother’s warmly arms
I found myself lost in the woods
Deep within depths of pain
In maze of love and deceit
As anger boils through my faded skin
Confusion reigns in tangled mind
Through the storm of whispered lies
A mother as canning as a serpent
Heart full of bitter cold lemon
As she breaks a strand of my heart

title: Ndayendamo Mnkhalango
content:
Ndithudi kuyenda mkuvina ndaonadi
zodengulisa moyo pa univesirty nde mzankhaninkhani
Koma sizimabwera usadakhesere thukuta
Poti munga umaza limodzi ndi mafinya.
Zakusimba saulula koma ine ndizikwerera pachulu
Nditambasure mazangazime ndaona ine
Kunotu sikufunika manja chontchololo uzakhara kapale moto wa sup
Koma kumafunika a zingalume amuna eni eni
Poti ma assignment amabwera chowilikiza ngati mafunde
Ogangarama motsitsisa utomoni wadiso mphongo.
Nde ukachita nawo bowa bwanga
Ndithu uzachita gombe utagunda mgolo.
Nawo mayeso safuna uzaya kunkhongo uzachita nguma
Koma afuna kulumira mlomo uzasimba lokoma.
Kukakhara kukwezedwa ngolo nde sitingavindikire
Poti anamwali kuno sadololoka mkuongoka kwako
Koma amaona mthumba ngati uli ndi khobidi
Moti kukhara opanda wakumkeka ife
Timaonera momwemu mwambi wa gulugufe.
Pena ukaombera mapila pamutu kumpeza wakumkeka
Umazaona chidameta mduliro nyani
Anamageya anyeziwa atakutengera nkhali ndi makobidi awo.
Nawo maluzi kuno ndi chimkukuludzi sasiya munthu.
Ndthu moyo wakuno ngwathumbozi wokharira kuthe.

title: University Campus Life
content:
Ravens enjoy their feast in the sinks and overflowing bins during the day
noise prowls beyond every hostel walls
dogs are our secret guards at night
every student sleeps like a hunted hare
no one come to help them
alone, each student struggles to secure his seat
Yet back home, they are proud of our sacrifice
that we will rescue them from untold starvation
but the campus beauty is every student’s checkpoint
many withdraw from pressure, poverty, depression and despair
every munite is our battle to go beyond the locked gates
still ravens watch and dogs keep guard
whoever endures and reaches that crossing line
is a role model for the morden life

title: Harvest of Dust
content:
You have come on time with notes in your hands
Your promises spoken yet nothing has grown
The river carried nothing of what you planned,
And left Mississippi delta bare and alone
We now know your rich mouth, full yet it deceives
You chew many words but feeds nothing true
The Canan you showed us ripened in leaves
But turns into dust where hope once grew
You have come to time when answers are due
When silence can no longer cover your name
The fire you planted still burns through and through
No ocean can quench or quite the flame
You have come to the hour you can not escape,
Mr president,
And stand with the weight of every mistake.

title: Political Struggle
content:
What politicians struggle for?
They waste their money and time for campaigns
Showering t-shirts, soaps and salt to people
Trying to prove a good leadership in them
As an athlete trying to win race
Sun scorch, snow falls on their head during political rallies
When they get the position they want
Everything in them changes as chameleon
Then people realize, they sold their rights and development
Exchanged with soaps, salt and t-shirts they received
Then people feel sorry for their fingers
They dipped into ink during elections
The question is, if this is not good leadership they struggle for
Then what politician struggle for?

title: My Life at Campus
content:
You ate my peace, scattered my brain into pieces
As if you had no idea that it was my first days at university campus
Why betraying my first semester?
I really faced unseen circumstances, which fooled my innocence
You squeezed life out of my body
You turned MUST blocks that seemed to be paradise
Into hell, burning with the fire of slides
You turned my expectations into a puzzle of wrong pieces
Your first week allowed me to dream
Dreams that seemed real,
Showed me the calm halls and welcoming skies
The skies full of people with bright smiles
Is this truly what I worked for? Yes!
Through storms I have found a stronger me
Life at campus shaped me in ways I never knew
Building strength, purpose, and future in view

title: At the Balcony of Hall Two
content:
At the balcony of Hall two
the sparkling pink of the administration block lingers through the eye
a dazzling quadrangle
architecture in unison
who built this place?
simply an arcadia of excellence

At the balcony of Hall two
unprompted joy flushes
then is overriden by academic pressure
assignments, test, projets all closing in
my stomach starts to rumble
I hate it here when am here
and crave it when am not

title: Deception
content:
No words from the victim can justify it
Facial expression trying hard to complement it
The eyes fearfully acting as backup
Body freezing from clear truth and reality
Attempt to turn lies into facts
Its outcome being ether fruitful or destructive
With the aim of rescuing oneself from a bitter truth
Every day and everywhere is filled with its smell
Bringing confusion between love and sympathy
For others, it is only the path to take
To some, it is the part of them, guiding and controlling them
Its strangest character is that, it can either hurt or construct
It is meant to dealt with care and purity
To escape its backstabbing, that’s it known by a name Deception.
